channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for talks with Iranian negotiators amid rising Strait of Hormuz tensions
item.description: Iran's military said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to continued Israeli military strikes in Lebanon; the U.S. said it remains open.

item.title: AI buildout gives tech investors new reasons to watch bond market
item.description: Tech giants are depleting cash reserves and raising debt in their ambitious data center buildouts, a dynamic that's forcing investors to watch interest rates.

item.title: The budget airline model in the U.S. is running out of runway
item.description: Spirit's bankruptcy as United and Delta fly high suggests there's more to success in airline industry than low fares, and failure isn't about jet fuel spikes.

item.title: SNAP restrictions could change what shoppers buy — and food giants are watching
item.description: SNAP food restrictions are spreading to more states, pressuring major food and beverage as consumers shift spending away from soda, candy and processed foods.

item.title: Bitcoin's future as revolutionary as the smartphone, according to CoinDesk
item.description: CoinDesk’s president of indices and data has a message for investors: Don’t count out bitcoin.

item.title: Five top tech stocks with major upside, Bank of America says
item.description: Bank of America reveals its top tech stocks heading into the second half.

item.title: Report says UK PM Starmer ready to quit, but source says he is still focused on the job
item.description: The Observer report said Starmer was discussing the matter with his wife at his Chequers country residence before making a final decision.

item.title: Musk's SpaceX stake is worth over $1 trillion. Here are the other billionaire shareholders
item.description: SpaceX shares were up 37% after its historic debut last week, which offered shares at a set price of $135.

item.title: Memory crisis hits such extremes that 'even Apple can't be safe'
item.description: Apple appears poised to take the rare step of increasing prices to deal with what CEO Tim Cook called an "unsustainable" memory shortage.

item.title: Goldman Sachs is betting that a recent merger will drive sharp gains for this travel stock
item.description: The bank this week upgraded the airline to buy from neutral.

item.title: Massive fire destroys resort in Dominican Republic and forces evacuation of almost 1,700 tourists
item.description: The fire forced the evacuation of nearly 1,700 tourists and caused the death of an Italian national, authorities said.

item.title: Trump unveils the new Air Force One, a converted Qatari jet
item.description: The gift from Qatar is serving as a so-called "bridge" aircraft to carry the president until the new planes ordered directly from Boeing arrive.

item.title: Trump tries to blame Reflecting Pool woes on vandalism, without offering substantiation
item.description: The paint is peeling from Washington's Reflecting Pool after the renovation ordered by President Donald Trump.

item.title: In photos: Star-studded crowd gathers to celebrate the Obama Presidential Center dedication
item.description: Barack Obama opened his presidential center in Chicago with former presidents, celebrities and dignitaries in attendance. President Donald Trump was absent.

item.title: 'Fun dads' get 5 things right about parenting that many people forget—'they're worth borrowing' this Father's Day, says expert
item.description: Many parents spend their days managing schedules, chores and responsibilities. This Father's Day, a parenting expert shares five "fun dad" habits that can increase joy and strengthen parent-child relationships.

item.title: Trump tells Axios he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat
item.description: Anthropic last week disabled access to its most advanced models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after Trump ordered it to block foreign ​nationals from accessing them.

item.title: DOJ rebuffs judge's request to put in writing it won't move forward with 'anti-weaponization' fund
item.description: The DOJ in May announced it was creating the fund as part of a settlement of President Donald Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service.

item.title: Iran to lodge FIFA complaint over World Cup travel restrictions
item.description: The Iran team are commuting from their tournament base in co-host Mexico for their group games in the U.S. due to visa uncertainties and the war.

item.title: Kennedy Center says it isn't required to reschedule shows after judge blocks 2-year closure
item.description: Kennedy Center lawyers said the institution plans to "maintain an operational model" after July 5, when it initially was scheduled to shutter for renovations.

item.title: Massive bonuses for South Korea's chip workers puts central bank on inflation alert
item.description: Workers from tech industries receive bonus worth millions of won, prompting the Bank of Korea to warn of the upward pressure to inflation.

item.title: Fedspeak vs. war deal: Here are the things that drove this week's stock market
item.description: The S&P 500 surged on Thursday, making up for losses seen after central bankers convened.

item.title: You can expect gas prices to remain elevated. Here's how to save
item.description: Maximize savings at the pump with these retail memberships, wholesalers and rewards credit cards.

item.title: The riskiest SpaceX stock trade of all had a big first week
item.description: SpaceX's IPO did not just mint the biggest debut in market history and a trillionaire, but it also triggered a historic land grab in risky leveraged ETFs.

item.title: NetJets' first fatal crash kills influential Texas VC founder
item.description: NetJets is not speculating on what caused one of its planes to crash onto a highway in Laredo, Texas late Tuesday, killing a prominent tech entrepreneur.

item.title: Struggling to find a job? Try looking in Nevada
item.description: The sparsely populated state known for its world-class casinos and dry climate has been a hiring hot spot.

item.title: John Jumper to leave Google DeepMind for Anthropic
item.description: Tech giants including Meta and Alphabet, along with upstarts such as Anthropic and OpenAI, are locked in a talent war as they race to build next-gen AI systems.

item.title: This nuclear stock will benefit from AI datacenter buildout and soaring energy demand, Roth Capital says
item.description: Stock in Nano Nuclear Energy should appreciate as it nears commercialization of its reactor plans while energy demand is surging, Roth Capital said.

item.title: Meloni slams Trump's claim she 'begged' for a photo with him as Italy's top diplomat cancels U.S. trip
item.description: Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani abruptly canceled a planned trip to the U.S., calling Trump's claims "serious and offensive" toward Meloni and Italy.

item.title: Bob Iger reflects on 10 years of Shanghai Disneyland as it defies the Chinese pullback
item.description: Shanghai Disneyland hit 100 million cumulative visitors in 2025, according to the company. It's a relatively new but important foothold in Disney's history.

item.title: Homeowners tapped $47 billion in equity in the first quarter. What to consider before you borrow
item.description: While high home values mean homeowners are sitting on an estimated $11 trillion in equity, it should not be viewed as free money, experts say.
